SEARCH
Package de.jvstvshd.necrify.api.user

Interface CommandSender

All Known Subinterfaces:
NecrifyUser
All Known Implementing Classes:
AbstractConsoleUser, VelocityConsoleUser, VelocityUser
public interface CommandSender
Represents an entity that is able to interact with the server via messages and may send commands to it. It is also able to hold permissions.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    boolean
    hasPermission(@NotNull String permission)
    Checks whether the command sender has a certain permission.
    void
    sendErrorMessage()
    Sends an error message to the command sender.

    Sends a message to the command sender.

    Sends a message to the command sender.

  • Method Details

    • sendMessage

      void sendMessage(@NotNull @NotNull net.kyori.adventure.text.Component message)
      Sends a message to the command sender. The message may be given in form of a TranslatableComponent, which will be translated to the correct language when being displayed.
      Parameters:
      message - a non-null component that represents the message to be sent.

    • sendMessage

      default void sendMessage(@NotNull @NotNull String key, net.kyori.adventure.text.Component... args)
      Sends a message to the command sender. The message must contain a valid translation key that is present in the language files of the server. The message will be translated to the correct language when being displayed, if a translation is available. If this key does not map to a translation, the key itself will be displayed. The base message (excluding any placeholders) will be colored with NamedTextColor.WHITE.

      Per default, this will use MessageProvider.provide(String, Component...)

      Parameters:
      key - a non-null string that represents the translation key of the message to be sent.
      args - the arguments to be passed to the translation to fill placeholders.

    • sendMessage

      void sendMessage(@NotNull @NotNull String key, net.kyori.adventure.text.format.TextColor color, net.kyori.adventure.text.Component... args)
      Sends a message to the command sender. The message must contain a valid translation key that is present in the language files of the server. The message will be translated to the correct language when being displayed, if a translation is available. If this key does not map to a translation, the key itself will be displayed. The base message (excluding any placeholders) will be colored with the given color.

      Per default, this will use MessageProvider.provide(String, Component...)

      Parameters:
      key - a non-null string that represents the translation key of the message to be sent.
      color - the color to be used for the base message.
      args - the arguments to be passed to the translation to fill placeholders.

    • sendErrorMessage

      void sendErrorMessage()
      Sends an error message to the command sender. This should be used to inform the command sender about an error that happened within the command execution, e.g. while updating data in the database. Ideally, this yields the same result as sendMessage("error.internal") content-wise, but may differ style-wise.
      See Also:
      • sendMessage(String, Component...)
      • MessageProvider.internalError()
      • MessageProvider.internalError(Locale)

    • hasPermission

      boolean hasPermission(@NotNull @NotNull String permission)
      Checks whether the command sender has a certain permission. This requires a permission system to be set up and the entity in question to exist.
      Parameters:
      permission - a non-null string that represents the permission to be checked.
      Returns:
      true if the command sender has the permission, false otherwise.
